children.full_sentence: The Jiva (ego) urged to actions by its past virtuous and sinful Karmas whirls about in this great Chakra of twelve spokes, so long as it does not grasp the truth.

children.name: Ekakshara Upanishad

children.full_sentence: Thou art He whom in the sacrifices the knowers of Brahman adore with Vedic Songs, formulas and rites and the soma juice; as the final 407 --- Page 408 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA (Truth) devoid of all admixture, as the golden, the best of Veda- knowers.

children.full_sentence: the truth in respect to the rule of the Tripundram (a sect mark consisting of three streaks) and what material, which place, how much, of what extent and which streaks, which divinity, which formula, which powers and which reward there are?” 419 --- Page 420 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA The exalted one said to him “The material should be the ash of fires.

children.full_sentence: Om Satyam.

children.full_sentence: Hari Om Tat Sat !

children.name: Katha Upanishad
O, thou art rooted in truth.
children.full_sentence: O, thou art rooted in truth.

children.full_sentence: As mover (sun), He dwells in heaven; (as air), He pervades everything and dwells in inter-space; as fire, on the earth; as guest, in 436 --- Page 437 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA the houses; He dwells in men; dwells in the gods; dwells in truth and dwells in space.

children.name: Without the grace of a good (perfect) guru, the abandonment of sensual objects is very difficult of ...
children.full_sentence: Without the grace of a good (perfect) guru, the abandonment of sensual objects is very difficult of attainment; so also the perception of (divine) truth and the attainment of one’s true state.

children.name: I am that Para-Brahman, which is the eternal, the immaculate, the free, the one, the undivided bliss...
children.full_sentence: I am that Para-Brahman, which is the eternal, the immaculate, the free, the one, the undivided bliss, the non-dual, the truth, the wisdom and the endless.

children.name: Know all to be happiness through the annihilation of sorrow and all to be of the nature of Sat (be-n...
children.full_sentence: Know all to be happiness through the annihilation of sorrow and all to be of the nature of Sat (be-ness) through the annihilation of Asat (not-be-ness).

children.name: Practise always silence ‘I am (viz., that you yourself are) Para- Brahman’ which is truth and absolu...
children.full_sentence: Practise always silence ‘I am (viz., that you yourself are) Para- Brahman’ which is truth and absolute Consciousness, which is undivided and non-dual, which is invisible, which is stainless, which is pure, which is second-less and which is beneficent.

children.name: Always practise silence – I am (viz., you are) the Brahman, that is the full, the secondless, the un...
children.full_sentence: Always practise silence – I am (viz., you are) the Brahman, that is the full, the secondless, the undivided consciousness which has neither 672 --- Page 673 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA the relationship nor the differences existing in the universe and which partakes of the essence of the non-dual and the supreme Sat and Chit.

children.name: That which always is and that which preserves the same nature during the three periods of time, unaf...
children.full_sentence: That which always is and that which preserves the same nature during the three periods of time, unaffected by anything, is my eternal form of Sat.

children.name: I am Brahman that is Chit, Sat and Ananda alone and that is Janardana (Vishnu).
children.full_sentence: I am Brahman that is Chit, Sat and Ananda alone and that is Janardana (Vishnu).

children.name: Katharudra Upanishad
That Brahman, which is without a second, and which is Truth, Knowledge and Happiness, is the object ...
children.full_sentence: That Brahman, which is without a second, and which is Truth, Knowledge and Happiness, is the object of (real) knowing.

children.name: By realizing directly this Brahman, which is named the Support (the Tail puccha), which is of the na...
children.full_sentence: By realizing directly this Brahman, which is named the Support (the Tail puccha), which is of the nature of truth, knowledge and non- duality, the essence, the joy, the eternal, the dweller in the body becomes happy everywhere.

children.name: Realizing that Brahman which is Bliss, without a second, devoid of attributes, the solidarity of tru...
children.full_sentence: Realizing that Brahman which is Bliss, without a second, devoid of attributes, the solidarity of truth and consciousness, as one’s own Self, one fears nought.

children.name: Narayana Upanishad
Whosoever among created beings sees that Brahman named Rita or ‘the True’, unintermittently pervadin...
children.full_sentence: Whosoever among created beings sees that Brahman named Rita or ‘the True’, unintermittently pervading the creation like the thread of a cloth, by contemplation in mind, truly becomes That.

children.name: Being such, grant us food, and great and beautiful insight (of the Supreme Truth).
children.full_sentence: Being such, grant us food, and great and beautiful insight (of the Supreme Truth).

children.name: Truth is austerity.
children.full_sentence: Truth is austerity.

children.full_sentence: XII-6: That which is the sun who abides in the clear sky, is the Vasu (the air that moves) in the mid-region, is the fire that dwells in the sacrificial altar and in the domestic hearth as the guest, is the fire that shines in men and in the gods, as the Soul, is the fire that is consecrated in the sacrifice, is dwelling in the sky as air, is born in water as submarine heat, is born in the rays of the sun, is the fire that is directly seen as the luminary, and is born on the mountain as the risi

children.name: XV-1: The sun alone is verily all these: -- energy, splendour, strength, renown, sight, hearing, bod...
children.full_sentence: XV-1: The sun alone is verily all these: -- energy, splendour, strength, renown, sight, hearing, body, mind, anger, Seer, the Deities Death, Satya, Mitra, Wind, Ether and Breath, the Rulers of the world, Prajapati, the Indeterminable One, happiness, that which transcends the senses, truth, food, (span of life), liberation or Immortality, individual Soul, the Universe, the acme of bliss and the self-born Brahman.

children.name: Gods worship Him as Tapas and Truth.
children.full_sentence: Gods worship Him as Tapas and Truth.

children.name: Truth is water.
children.full_sentence: Truth is water.

children.name: Further whatever sinful deed has been committed by me, all that and myself I offer as an oblation in...
children.full_sentence: Further whatever sinful deed has been committed by me, all that and myself I offer as an oblation into the Self-luminous Truth, the source of Immortality.

children.name: Thou art the Truth denoted by the Pranava.
children.full_sentence: Thou art the Truth denoted by the Pranava.

children.name: Om Abode of Truth.
children.full_sentence: Om Abode of Truth.

children.name: He is the Truth.
children.full_sentence: He is the Truth.

children.name: Gods worship Him as Tapas and Truth.
children.full_sentence: Gods worship Him as Tapas and Truth.

children.name: XXXIX-4: To me, who is the devotee of the Supreme Truth let the wind blow sweetly.
children.full_sentence: XXXIX-4: To me, who is the devotee of the Supreme Truth let the wind blow sweetly.

children.full_sentence: XXXX-3: That which is the sun who abides in the clear sky, is the Vasu (the air that moves) in the mid-region, is the fire that dwells in the sacrificial altar and in the domestic hearth as the guest, is the fire that shines in men and in the gods, as the Soul, is the fire that is consecrated in the sacrifice, is dwelling in the sky as air, is born in water as submarine heat, is born in the rays of the sun, is the fire that is directly seen as the luminary, and is born on the mountain as the ris

children.name: XLI-1: May the all-penetrating goddess of intellect who is beneficial, favourably disposed to, and d...
children.full_sentence: XLI-1: May the all-penetrating goddess of intellect who is beneficial, favourably disposed to, and delighting in, us visit us, O goddess, may we who were delighting in profitless speech before thy visit, now as the result of thy delight in us, become enlightened and also capable of expressing the Supreme Truth along with our heroic sons and disciples.

children.name: (I am the Truth expressed by Pranava.
children.full_sentence: (I am the Truth expressed by Pranava.

children.name: Om that is the Supreme Truth.
children.full_sentence: Om that is the Supreme Truth.

children.name: LXXVIII-1: Truthfulness is excellent.
children.full_sentence: LXXVIII-1: Truthfulness is excellent.

children.name: What is excellent is truthfulness only.
children.full_sentence: What is excellent is truthfulness only.

children.name: By truthfulness those who have attained to the state of blissfulness never fall from there.
children.full_sentence: By truthfulness those who have attained to the state of blissfulness never fall from there.

children.name: What belongs to sat, namely good people, that is indeed satyam (truthfulness).
children.full_sentence: What belongs to sat, namely good people, that is indeed satyam (truthfulness).

children.name: For this reason seekers of the highest good find delight in truthfulness.
children.full_sentence: For this reason seekers of the highest good find delight in truthfulness.

children.name: To him Prajapati thus replied: LXXIX-2: By truth the wind blows.
children.full_sentence: To him Prajapati thus replied: LXXIX-2: By truth the wind blows.

children.name: By truth the sun shines in the sky.
children.full_sentence: By truth the sun shines in the sky.

children.name: Truth is the foundation of speech.
children.full_sentence: Truth is the foundation of speech.

children.name: Everything in practical life depends on truth.
children.full_sentence: Everything in practical life depends on truth.

children.name: Therefore they say truth is the supreme means of liberation.
children.full_sentence: Therefore they say truth is the supreme means of liberation.

children.name: As the result of such Tapas, faith in scriptural truths springs into existence.
children.full_sentence: As the result of such Tapas, faith in scriptural truths springs into existence.

children.name: Conclusive experience of Truth follows calmness.
children.full_sentence: Conclusive experience of Truth follows calmness.

children.name: By conclusive experience of Truth remembrance of It is engendered.
children.full_sentence: By conclusive experience of Truth remembrance of It is engendered.

children.name: From continuous remembrance results unbroken direct realization of Truth.
children.full_sentence: From continuous remembrance results unbroken direct realization of Truth.

children.name: For, it is found that the vital breaths and the senses of creatures are from food, that reflection f...
children.full_sentence: For, it is found that the vital breaths and the senses of creatures are from food, that reflection functions with the vital breath and the senses, that unbroken direct realization comes from reflection and that bliss comes from unbroken direct realization of Truth.

children.name: He is firmly established in the richness of knowledge imparted by his guru, as also in his faith and...
children.full_sentence: He is firmly established in the richness of knowledge imparted by his guru, as also in his faith and in Truth.

children.name: Hridaya Upanishad
It deals with that supreme philosophy of the Absolute Truth, ununderstandable, impersonal, Nirguna, ...
children.full_sentence: It deals with that supreme philosophy of the Absolute Truth, ununderstandable, impersonal, Nirguna, Nirakara, without ears, without eyes, without hands, without feet, eternal, omnipresent, imperishable, and knowable by the intelligent daring sages.

children.name: This is the eternal Truth.
children.full_sentence: This is the eternal Truth.

children.name: All is Truth.
children.full_sentence: All is Truth.

children.name: This is the real wisdom to be understood by an aspirant seeking after Truth.
children.full_sentence: This is the real wisdom to be understood by an aspirant seeking after Truth.

children.name: Think that you are the basis of all this universe, you are the One, Kevala, Sat-Chit-Ghana.
children.full_sentence: Think that you are the basis of all this universe, you are the One, Kevala, Sat-Chit-Ghana.

children.name: All people cannot understand this Truth.
children.full_sentence: All people cannot understand this Truth.

children.name: One who knows this great secret Truth is the real Muni.
children.full_sentence: One who knows this great secret Truth is the real Muni.

children.name: Tell us that, Holy Sir, by meditation on which you know the Truth.
children.full_sentence: Tell us that, Holy Sir, by meditation on which you know the Truth.

children.name: The sages said: How, by what meditation, Sage of Dedicated Life, 532 --- Page 533 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-...
children.full_sentence: The sages said: How, by what meditation, Sage of Dedicated Life, 532 --- Page 533 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA is the truth of Sarasvati won ?

children.name: Sarasvati-Rahasya Upanishad
BLUM Inspirer of truthful words, Awakener of noble minds, Sarasvati receives worship.
children.full_sentence: BLUM Inspirer of truthful words, Awakener of noble minds, Sarasvati receives worship.

children.name: Sarasvati thus spake: Through Me even Brahma won 543 --- Page 544 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA Self-knowle...
children.full_sentence: Sarasvati thus spake: Through Me even Brahma won 543 --- Page 544 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA Self-knowledge eternal; Ever being Truth, Knowledge, Bliss, Mine is perpetual Brahmanhood, Without let or hindrance.

children.name: That Maya is adjunct, Wholly subordinate To all-knowing Ishwara; For, His alone oneness, Over Maya l...
children.full_sentence: That Maya is adjunct, Wholly subordinate To all-knowing Ishwara; For, His alone oneness, Over Maya lordship, and Omniscience are, in truth.

children.name: They are not real – who knows this, In truth, is free – doubt is there none.
children.full_sentence: They are not real – who knows this, In truth, is free – doubt is there none.

children.name: Non-violence, truth, non-theft, continence and non-possession, absence of anger, service to elders, ...
children.full_sentence: Non-violence, truth, non-theft, continence and non-possession, absence of anger, service to elders, cleanliness, contentment and honesty, non-conceit, candour, faith and non-injury – are the qualities (effects) of Sattva.

children.name: The twenty-three are the Tattvas (eternal verities) relating to Prakriti.
children.full_sentence: The twenty-three are the Tattvas (eternal verities) relating to Prakriti.

children.name: The twenty-fourth is the Avyakta, the chief (Tattva).
children.full_sentence: The twenty-fourth is the Avyakta, the chief (Tattva).

children.full_sentence: 565 --- Page 566 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA Then Shiva, hearing this sat in a divine seat to give instruction.

children.name: ‘Truth, knowledge, infinity is Brahman.
children.full_sentence: ‘Truth, knowledge, infinity is Brahman.

children.full_sentence: ‘Truth, knowledge, infinity is Brahman’ – Phat to the inside and outside of the palm.

children.name: ‘Truth, knowledge, infinity is Brahman – bow to the heart’.
children.full_sentence: ‘Truth, knowledge, infinity is Brahman – bow to the heart’.

children.name: Rahasya Upanishad
The truth of the universe is stated of all that is seen by the word Brahman.
children.full_sentence: The truth of the universe is stated of all that is seen by the word Brahman.

children.name: In ‘Tattvam Asi’ the expressed meaning is the senses etc., which are elemental and the implied is, ‘...
children.full_sentence: In ‘Tattvam Asi’ the expressed meaning is the senses etc., which are elemental and the implied is, ‘He’ in ‘you’ (Tvam), in the word Tat the expressed sense 569 --- Page 570 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA is lordship etc., the implied is the supreme being which is Sat, Chit and Ananda.

children.name: Tvam and Tad mean effect and cause respectively when this is the adjunct; otherwise both are the sam...
children.full_sentence: Tvam and Tad mean effect and cause respectively when this is the adjunct; otherwise both are the same Sat, Chit and Ananda – separating the space and time faites the identity is got, just as in the world, in the expression, ‘This is that Devadatta’.

children.name: Svetasvatara Upanishad
He who, by means of truthfulness, self-control and concentration, looks again and again for this Sel...
children.full_sentence: He who, by means of truthfulness, self-control and concentration, looks again and again for this Self, which is all- pervading like butter contained in milk, and which is rooted in self- knowledge and meditation – he becomes that Supreme Brahman, the destroyer of ignorance.

children.name: II-1: First harnessing the mind and the senses with a view to realizing the Truth, and then having f...
children.full_sentence: II-1: First harnessing the mind and the senses with a view to realizing the Truth, and then having found out the light of the fire, the Evolving Soul brought itself out of the earth.

children.name: II-14: Just as the same metal disc, which was stained by dust before, shines brilliantly when cleane...
children.full_sentence: II-14: Just as the same metal disc, which was stained by dust before, shines brilliantly when cleaned, so the embodied being, seeing the truth of Atman, realizes oneness, attains the goal and becomes sorrowless.

children.name: II-15: When the Yogin realizes the truth of Brahman, through the 577 --- Page 578 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-...
children.full_sentence: II-15: When the Yogin realizes the truth of Brahman, through the 577 --- Page 578 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA perception of the truth of Atman in this body as a self-luminous entity, then, knowing the Divinity as unborn, eternal and free from all the modifications of Prakriti, he is freed from all sins.

children.name: III-6: O revealer of the Vedic truths, deign to make propitious that arrow which Thou holdest in Thy...
children.full_sentence: III-6: O revealer of the Vedic truths, deign to make propitious that arrow which Thou holdest in Thy hand for shooting at somebody.

children.name: VI-21: Himself realizing Brahman by the power of self-control and concentration of mind, as well as ...
children.full_sentence: VI-21: Himself realizing Brahman by the power of self-control and concentration of mind, as well as by the grace of God, the sage Svetasvatara expounded well to the highest order of Sannyasins, the truth of that supremely holy Brahman resorted to by all the seers.

children.name: VI-23: These truths, when taught, shine forth only in that high-souled one who has supreme devotion ...
children.full_sentence: VI-23: These truths, when taught, shine forth only in that high-souled one who has supreme devotion to God, and an equal degree of devotion to the spiritual teacher.

children.name: I shall call you truth.
children.full_sentence: I shall call you truth.

children.name: Taittiriya Upanishad
Over and above all these he becomes Brahman which is embodied in Akasa, which is identified with the...
children.full_sentence: Over and above all these he becomes Brahman which is embodied in Akasa, which is identified with the gross and the subtle and has truth as Its real nature, which reveals in life, under whose possession the mind is a source of bliss, which is enriched with peace and is immortal.

children.name: Truth and learning and teaching (are to be practised).
children.full_sentence: Truth and learning and teaching (are to be practised).

children.name: Truth (is the thing) – this is what Satyavacha, of the line of Rathitara, thinks.
children.full_sentence: Truth (is the thing) – this is what Satyavacha, of the line of Rathitara, thinks.

children.full_sentence: I-xi-1: Having taught the Vedas, the preceptor imparts this post- instruction to the students: “Speak the truth.

children.name: There should be no inadvertence about truth.
children.full_sentence: There should be no inadvertence about truth.

children.name: I shall call you truth.
children.full_sentence: I shall call you truth.

children.name: Here is a verse uttering that very fact: “Brahman is truth, knowledge, and infinite.
children.full_sentence: Here is a verse uttering that very fact: “Brahman is truth, knowledge, and infinite.

children.name: Of him faith is verily the head; righteousness is the right side; truth is the left side; concentrat...
children.full_sentence: Of him faith is verily the head; righteousness is the right side; truth is the left side; concentration is the self (trunk); (the principle, called) Mahat, is the stabilising tail.

children.name: Truth became all this that there is.
children.full_sentence: Truth became all this that there is.

children.name: They call that Brahman Truth.
children.full_sentence: They call that Brahman Truth.

children.name: It is inconceivable, unknowable, non-truth and not the highest.
children.full_sentence: It is inconceivable, unknowable, non-truth and not the highest.

children.name: In Tyaga (renunciation), one abandons the manifestation (or objects) of the universe through the cog...
children.full_sentence: In Tyaga (renunciation), one abandons the manifestation (or objects) of the universe through the cognition of Atman that is Sat and Chit.

children.name: The Grandfather (Brahma) having saluted the Lord of the universe (Vishnu) and having paid Him due re...
children.full_sentence: The Grandfather (Brahma) having saluted the Lord of the universe (Vishnu) and having paid Him due respects, asked Him (thus): “Pray, explain to us the truth of Yoga which includes in it the eight 727 --- Page 728 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA subservients.” 4.

children.name: I shall explain its truth.
children.full_sentence: I shall explain its truth.

children.full_sentence: Maha-Mudra, Maha-Bandha and Khechari, Jalandhara, Uddiyana and Mula-Bandha, uttering without intermission Pranava (OM) for a long time and hearing the exposition of the supreme truths, 730 --- Page 731 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA Vajroli, Amaroli and Sahajoli, which form a triad – all these separately I shall give a true description of.

children.name: Tejo-Bindu Upanishad
When the cause ceases to exist in truth, the effect perishes through right discrimination.
children.full_sentence: When the cause ceases to exist in truth, the effect perishes through right discrimination.

children.name: Asat and Sat are Chinmatra.
children.full_sentence: Asat and Sat are Chinmatra.

children.name: Meditation, truth, sheaths and others, the (eight) Vasus, silence, non-silence and indifference to o...
children.full_sentence: Meditation, truth, sheaths and others, the (eight) Vasus, silence, non-silence and indifference to objects – are nothing without Chinmatra.

children.name: My real nature is indescribable, of endless bliss, the bliss above Sat and Chit and the interior of ...
children.full_sentence: My real nature is indescribable, of endless bliss, the bliss above Sat and Chit and the interior of the interior.

children.name: I am solely Sat, Ananda and Chit which is unconditioned and pure.
children.full_sentence: I am solely Sat, Ananda and Chit which is unconditioned and pure.

children.name: I am content in my own Atman, have no form, or no decay, am without, the number one, have the nature...
children.full_sentence: I am content in my own Atman, have no form, or no decay, am without, the number one, have the nature of an unconditioned and emancipated one, and I am subtler than Akasa; I am without the existence of beginning or end, of the nature of the all- 625 --- Page 626 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA illuminating, the bliss greater than the great, of the sole nature of Sat, of the nature of pure Moksha, of the nature of truth and bliss, full of spiritual wisdom and bliss, of the nature of wisdom alone and of the

children.name: I am Brahman that is Sat and bliss and the ancient.
children.full_sentence: I am Brahman that is Sat and bliss and the ancient.

children.name: I am the primeval consciousness alone, the partless and non-dual essence, beyond reach of speech and...
children.full_sentence: I am the primeval consciousness alone, the partless and non-dual essence, beyond reach of speech and mind, of the nature of bliss everywhere, of the nature of fullness everywhere, of the nature of earthly bliss, of the nature of contentment everywhere, the supreme nectary essence and the one and secondless Sat, (viz.,) Brahman.

children.name: I am of the nature of the emancipated and emancipation, of Nirvanic bliss, of truth and wisdom, of S...
children.full_sentence: I am of the nature of the emancipated and emancipation, of Nirvanic bliss, of truth and wisdom, of Sat alone and bliss, of the one beyond the fourth, of one without fancy and ever of the nature of Aja (the unborn).

children.name: The Murtis (Brahma, Vishnu and Rudra having limitation) are unreal, all the creation is unreal, all ...
children.full_sentence: The Murtis (Brahma, Vishnu and Rudra having limitation) are unreal, all the creation is unreal, all the Tattvas are unreal, but know that I am the great Sadashiva.

children.name: I am of the nature of Sat.
children.full_sentence: I am of the nature of Sat.

children.name: He is a Videhamukta who having given up the certainty of 632 --- Page 633 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA the...
children.full_sentence: He is a Videhamukta who having given up the certainty of 632 --- Page 633 --- KRISHNA-YAJUR-VEDA the existence or non-existence of all objects is pure Chidananda (the consciousness-bliss), who having abandoned (the thought): ‘I am Brahman’ (or) ‘I am not Brahman’ does not mingle his Atman with anything, anywhere or at any time, who is ever silent with the silence of Satya, who does nothing, who has gone beyond Gunas, whose Atman has become the All, the great and the purifier of the elements, who

children.name: He who has no body, nor is a Jiva made up of the elements and their compounds, who has neither form ...
children.full_sentence: He who has no body, nor is a Jiva made up of the elements and their compounds, who has neither form nor name, neither the enjoyable nor the enjoyer, neither Sat nor Asat, neither preservation nor regeneration, neither Guna nor non-Guna – that is undoubtedly my Atman.

children.name: Where there is truth alone, there is no non-truth possible; where there is non-truth alone, there is...
children.full_sentence: Where there is truth alone, there is no non-truth possible; where there is non-truth alone, there is no truth possible.

children.name: There is no Ganga, no Gaya, no Setu (bridge), no elements or anything else, no earth, water, fire, V...
children.full_sentence: There is no Ganga, no Gaya, no Setu (bridge), no elements or anything else, no earth, water, fire, Vayu and Akasa anywhere, no Devas, no guardians of the four quarters, no Vedas, no Guru, no distance, no proximity, no time, no middle, no non-duality, no truth, no untruth, no bondage, no emancipation, no Sat, no Asat, no happiness, etc., no class, no motion, no caste and no worldly business.

children.name: Whatever is determined by the Buddhi, whatever is cognised by Chitta, whatever is discussed by the r...
children.full_sentence: Whatever is determined by the Buddhi, whatever is cognised by Chitta, whatever is discussed by the religious books, whatever is seen by the eye and heard by the ears and whatever exists as Sat, as also the ear, the eye and the limbs – all these are unreal.

children.name: Whatever is uttered by words, whatever is ascertained, spoken, enjoyed, given or done by anyone, wha...
children.full_sentence: Whatever is uttered by words, whatever is ascertained, spoken, enjoyed, given or done by anyone, whatever action is done, good or bad, whatever is done as truth – know all these to be unreal.

children.name: Thou art the truth.
children.full_sentence: Thou art the truth.

children.name: Thou art impartial, Sat and the ancient knowledge, recognised by the words ‘Truth, etc.’.

children.full_sentence: One who has directly realized, like the berry in the palm, the Atman without a second, devoid of class, quality and action and of defects like the six waves (like hunger), the states (like birth and death), of the nature of truth, knowledge and bliss, free from adjuncts, the basis of all thoughts, immanent in all creatures, present inside and outside like space.

children.name: One should contemplate one’s self as the spirit without a second, truth, knowledge and bliss.
children.full_sentence: One should contemplate one’s self as the spirit without a second, truth, knowledge and bliss.

children.name: Atharvaveda
Annapurna Upanishad
Then, rising, that ascetic respectfully said, ‘Teach me the truth about the Self; by what kind of ad...
children.full_sentence: Then, rising, that ascetic respectfully said, ‘Teach me the truth about the Self; by what kind of adoration have you, Oh Brahmana, attained this state ?

children.name: Thus hidden by the wide-eyed (deity) I spoke: ‘O Daughter of the mountain, may the truth of the Self...
children.full_sentence: Thus hidden by the wide-eyed (deity) I spoke: ‘O Daughter of the mountain, may the truth of the Self dawn on my mind’.

children.name: This state of slumber, O Nidagha, fully developed through 1005 --- Page 1006 --- ATHARVA-VEDA practi...
children.full_sentence: This state of slumber, O Nidagha, fully developed through 1005 --- Page 1006 --- ATHARVA-VEDA practice, is styled the Fourth by the best knowers of Truth.

children.name: Ribhu: In the region of Sumeru the celebrated sage Mandavya resorting to Truth (imparted by) Kaundin...
children.full_sentence: Ribhu: In the region of Sumeru the celebrated sage Mandavya resorting to Truth (imparted by) Kaundinya became liberated in life.

children.name: He sat in the lotus-posture, with eyes half-closed, slowly avoiding contacts (with objects), externa...
children.full_sentence: He sat in the lotus-posture, with eyes half-closed, slowly avoiding contacts (with objects), external and internal.

children.name: Which is the All, the all-pervading Reality, the Truth.
children.full_sentence: Which is the All, the all-pervading Reality, the Truth.

children.name: That non- dual Brahman characterised by truth, knowledge, and bliss is the object of knowledge.
children.full_sentence: That non- dual Brahman characterised by truth, knowledge, and bliss is the object of knowledge.

children.name: Having the certain knowledge, ‘I am the ground of the whole world, solid Truth and Knowledge’, the s...
children.full_sentence: Having the certain knowledge, ‘I am the ground of the whole world, solid Truth and Knowledge’, the sage may dispel (all) sorrow.

children.name: That is the pure unobscured Truth of the Self; when that is known the mind is tranquillised.
children.full_sentence: That is the pure unobscured Truth of the Self; when that is known the mind is tranquillised.

children.name: O knower of Truth !
children.full_sentence: O knower of Truth !

children.name: As long as the latent impressions are not attenuated, the mind is not tranquillised; as long as the ...
children.full_sentence: As long as the latent impressions are not attenuated, the mind is not tranquillised; as long as the knowledge of truth is not won, whence can come mental tranquillity ?

children.name: As long as the mind is not tranquil, Truth cannot be known; so long as the knowledge of Truth is not...
children.full_sentence: As long as the mind is not tranquil, Truth cannot be known; so long as the knowledge of Truth is not won whence can mental tranquillity come ?

children.name: Knowledge of Truth, mind’s destruction, attenuation of latent impressions – (these) mutually cause o...
children.full_sentence: Knowledge of Truth, mind’s destruction, attenuation of latent impressions – (these) mutually cause one another; they are indeed hard to accomplish.

children.name: Sought for long and simultaneously, the attenuation of latent impressions, knowledge (of Truth), and...
children.full_sentence: Sought for long and simultaneously, the attenuation of latent impressions, knowledge (of Truth), and the destruction of the mind are held to prove effective.

children.name: Truth-knowers know that breath-control corresponds to the eschewal of latent impressions; therefore,...
children.full_sentence: Truth-knowers know that breath-control corresponds to the eschewal of latent impressions; therefore, also, practise this latter too, by breath control.

children.name: The mind of the knower is not called mind; mind indeed is the Truth of Spirit.
children.full_sentence: The mind of the knower is not called mind; mind indeed is the Truth of Spirit.

children.name: Atharvasikha Upanishad
I meditate on that “ultimate truth”, which explains the meaning “of the sound of Om”, which shines a...
children.full_sentence: I meditate on that “ultimate truth”, which explains the meaning “of the sound of Om”, which shines as the fourth leg of Om, which is Thureeya of Thureeyas (A super divine state of oneness with God, which is beyond the three states of wakefulness, sleep and dream), which enjoys being in three legs (syllables) and which is one and only 1036 --- Page 1037 --- ATHARVA-VEDA one.

children.name: Atharvasiras Upanishad
He is truth and we salute him again and again.
children.full_sentence: He is truth and we salute him again and again.

children.name: They who worship “penance” and “truth”, get permanent happiness.
children.full_sentence: They who worship “penance” and “truth”, get permanent happiness.

children.name: Atma Upanishad
From the point of view of truth, pure Brahman alone is.
children.full_sentence: From the point of view of truth, pure Brahman alone is.

children.name: Because it appears to be encompassed by Rahu (the darkness), the unencompassed sun is said to be enc...
children.full_sentence: Because it appears to be encompassed by Rahu (the darkness), the unencompassed sun is said to be encompassed by deluded men, not knowing the truth.

children.name: Bondage and liberation may be described as real and unreal and as due to the nescience (concealment ...
children.full_sentence: Bondage and liberation may be described as real and unreal and as due to the nescience (concealment of truth).

children.name: In the supreme Truth as in the sky, impartite, inactive, quiescent, flawless, unstained and non-dual...
children.full_sentence: In the supreme Truth as in the sky, impartite, inactive, quiescent, flawless, unstained and non-dual where is room for (mental) construction ?

children.name: Neither suppression nor generation, neither the bond nor the striving: neither the liberty seeking n...
children.full_sentence: Neither suppression nor generation, neither the bond nor the striving: neither the liberty seeking nor the liberated – this is the 1053 --- Page 1054 --- ATHARVA-VEDA metaphysical truth.

children.name: Jabala Upanishad
This is the highest truth and the greatest instruction.
children.full_sentence: This is the highest truth and the greatest instruction.

children.name: I am the Highest Truth that exists, the Brahman of the Upanishads.
children.full_sentence: I am the Highest Truth that exists, the Brahman of the Upanishads.

children.name: This Upanishad ends with the blessing ”Om Sathya!”(long live the truth).
children.full_sentence: This Upanishad ends with the blessing ”Om Sathya!”(long live the truth).

children.name: Atharva Upanishad
You clearly are the tattva.
children.full_sentence: You clearly are the tattva.

children.name: The truth I speak.
children.full_sentence: The truth I speak.

children.full_sentence: “Control of senses” was born as Sage Udhawa and “Truth” was born as Sage Akroora.

children.name: Mandukya Upanishad
Prajna knows neither himself nor others, neither truth nor untruth.
children.full_sentence: Prajna knows neither himself nor others, neither truth nor untruth.

children.name: When (the Truth is) realised, duality does not exist.
children.full_sentence: When (the Truth is) realised, duality does not exist.

children.name: This is the ultimate truth.
children.full_sentence: This is the ultimate truth.

children.name: Thus know the knowers of Truth.
children.full_sentence: Thus know the knowers of Truth.

children.name: Having perceived Truth internally and having perceived it externally, one should become identified w...
children.full_sentence: Having perceived Truth internally and having perceived it externally, one should become identified with Truth, should derive delight from Truth, and should never deviate from Truth III.

children.name: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad
When the mind ceases to imagine consequent on the realisation of the Truth which is the Self, then i...
children.full_sentence: When the mind ceases to imagine consequent on the realisation of the Truth which is the Self, then it attains the state of not being the mind and becomes a non-perceiver, owing to the absence of objects to be perceived.

children.name: (Such being the case), this is the highest Truth where nothing is born whatsoever.
children.full_sentence: (Such being the case), this is the highest Truth where nothing is born whatsoever.

children.name: This is that supreme truth where nothing is born whatsoever.
children.full_sentence: This is that supreme truth where nothing is born whatsoever.

children.name: Having realised the Truth that is uncaused and having abstained from obtaining any further cause, on...
children.full_sentence: Having realised the Truth that is uncaused and having abstained from obtaining any further cause, one attains the state of fearlessness that is devoid of grief and delusion (kama).

children.name: Mundaka Upanishad
I-ii-9: Continuing diversely in the midst of ignorance, the unenlightened take airs by thinking, ‘We...
children.full_sentence: I-ii-9: Continuing diversely in the midst of ignorance, the unenlightened take airs by thinking, ‘We have attained the goal.’ Since the men, engaged in karma, do not understand (the truth) under the influence of attachment, thereby they become afflicted with sorrow and are deprived of heaven on the exhaustion of the results of karma.

children.name: II-i-7: And from Him duly emerged the gods in various groups, the Sadhyas, human beings, beasts, bir...
children.full_sentence: II-i-7: And from Him duly emerged the gods in various groups, the Sadhyas, human beings, beasts, birds, life, rice and barley, as well as austerity, faith, truth, continence and dutifulness.

children.name: III-i-5: The bright and pure Self within the body, that the monks with (habitual effort and) attenua...
children.full_sentence: III-i-5: The bright and pure Self within the body, that the monks with (habitual effort and) attenuated blemishes see, is attainable verily through truth, concentration, complete knowledge, and continence, practised constantly.

children.name: III-i-6: Truth alone wins, and not untruth.
children.full_sentence: III-i-6: Truth alone wins, and not untruth.

children.name: By truth is laid the path called Devayana, by which the desireless seers ascend to where exists the ...
children.full_sentence: By truth is laid the path called Devayana, by which the desireless seers ascend to where exists the supreme treasure attainable through truth.

children.name: III-ii-10: This (rule) has been revealed by the mantra (which runs thus): ‘To them alone should one ...
children.full_sentence: III-ii-10: This (rule) has been revealed by the mantra (which runs thus): ‘To them alone should one expound this knowledge of b who are engaged in the practice of disciplines, versed in the Vedas, and indeed devoted to Brahman, who personally sacrifice to the fire called Ekarsi with faith, and by whom has been duly accomplished the vow of holding fire on the head.’ III-ii-11: The seer Angiras spoke of this Truth in the days of yore.

children.name: Parivrajaka Upanishad
He is in the stage (leading to) final beatitude in whom are found peace, quiescence, purity, truth, ...
children.full_sentence: He is in the stage (leading to) final beatitude in whom are found peace, quiescence, purity, truth, contentment, straightforwardness, absence of any possession and false airs.

children.name: The ten virtues characterising right conduct (dharma) are: Contentment, forgiveness, self-control, n...
children.full_sentence: The ten virtues characterising right conduct (dharma) are: Contentment, forgiveness, self-control, non-stealing, purity, control of the senses, humility, (scriptural) learning, truth, and an even temper.

children.name: That ascetic is said to be tongueless who, while eating food is not conscious of its being savoury o...
children.full_sentence: That ascetic is said to be tongueless who, while eating food is not conscious of its being savoury or otherwise and who speaks words beneficial and truthful in moderation.

children.name: The Yogin, the knower of yoga and the perceiver of the truth, shall possess that sutra (Brahman) by ...
children.full_sentence: The Yogin, the knower of yoga and the perceiver of the truth, shall possess that sutra (Brahman) by which everything is held together (sustained), as a group of gems by a thread.

children.name: An ascetic comes down (from his high position) if he has greed, anger, untruth, bamboozling, covetou...
children.full_sentence: An ascetic comes down (from his high position) if he has greed, anger, untruth, bamboozling, covetousness, delusion, likes and dislikes, (love of) fine art, explaining tendency, desire, passion, accepting (gifts), egotism, ‘mine-ness’, practising medicine, enforcing right conduct (in others), expiatory acts, journeying abroad (into unholy places), and (the practice of) mantras, herbs, poisons and blessing (others) which are prohibited.

children.name: Abstaining from injury, (having) truthfulness, non-stealing, continence, non-acquiring, humility, ab...
children.full_sentence: Abstaining from injury, (having) truthfulness, non-stealing, continence, non-acquiring, humility, absence of depression, serenity, stability, straight-forwardness, freedom from affection, serving 1199 --- Page 1200 --- ATHARVA-VEDA respectable elders, faith, forgiveness, calmness, non-alignment, fortitude, amiability, endurance, compassion, modesty, knowledge and wisdom, contemplation of the supreme Spirit (Yoga), very moderate diet and contentment – these are well known as the essential requisi

children.name: First (he shall propitiate) the group of deities named Satya, Vasu, etc.; in the commemorative cerem...
children.full_sentence: First (he shall propitiate) the group of deities named Satya, Vasu, etc.; in the commemorative ceremony for the gods, Brahma, Vishnu and Maheshvara (Siva); in the Sraddha ceremony for the sages, divine sages (like Narada), royal sages (like Janaka) and men-sages (like Yajnavalkya); in the Sraddha for divine beings, the (eight) Vasus, the (eleven) Rudras and the (twelve) Adityas (suns); in the Sraddha for men, Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanatkumara and Sanatsujata (son of the god Brahma); in the Sraddha

children.name: ‘The individual soul, non-different from Brahman, pervading pure ether, the sun (Vasu), remaining in...
children.full_sentence: ‘The individual soul, non-different from Brahman, pervading pure ether, the sun (Vasu), remaining in the sky, the Fire that rests in the altar (of the universe), the Guest, residing in the house (of the sacrifice), residing in men, dwelling in the superior (gods), resting in truth, residing in the sky (as the sun), born in the waters, born on earth (as grain, etc.,) born as (sacrificial) truth, born in mountains (as rivers), this truth (Brahman) is (truly) great.

children.name: He shall not put on airs nor speak an untruth.
children.full_sentence: He shall not put on airs nor speak an untruth.

children.name: The Paramahamsa reaches the abode of Brahma and of Truth (Satyaloka).
children.full_sentence: The Paramahamsa reaches the abode of Brahma and of Truth (Satyaloka).

children.name: That ascetic is considered as one beyond the castes and orders (ativarnashramin) who realizes the su...
children.full_sentence: That ascetic is considered as one beyond the castes and orders (ativarnashramin) who realizes the supreme truth which is free of the body, senses, etc., which is the all-witness, the spiritual wisdom, the self of bliss and the self-radiant.

children.name: The knowers of the truth of all the Vedas declare that man to be beyond the castes and orders who is...
children.full_sentence: The knowers of the truth of all the Vedas declare that man to be beyond the castes and orders who is established in his Self, having reached the stage beyond his order (ashrama) an his caste (varna).

children.name: Pleasing him by rendering personal service attentively for a long time, he shall always listen with ...
children.full_sentence: Pleasing him by rendering personal service attentively for a long time, he shall always listen with great attention to (his exposition of) the truths of the Upanishads.

children.name: The wise ascetic, the Yogin, his mind dwelling on the highest truth, shall not destroy insects, worm...
children.full_sentence: The wise ascetic, the Yogin, his mind dwelling on the highest truth, shall not destroy insects, worms, moths, as well as trees.

children.name: Having considered himself as separate (as the individual Self, as the ether in the pot with referenc...
children.full_sentence: Having considered himself as separate (as the individual Self, as the ether in the pot with reference to the all- pervading ether), and Brahman as the controlling Self (he is revolved in the wheel of worldly life); and then becoming beloved by Him (on realizing the truth in 'Thou art That', and ‘I am Brahman’) (the individual soul) attains immortality.

children.name: The First Upanishad 1250 --- Page 1251 --- ATHARVA-VEDA Bhagawan Narasimha who is partly human and p...
children.full_sentence: The First Upanishad 1250 --- Page 1251 --- ATHARVA-VEDA Bhagawan Narasimha who is partly human and partly lion shines as Parabrahma with Rutha (discipline of the visible world) and Sathya (basic truth).

children.name: Second Upanishad
It is the truth.
children.full_sentence: It is the truth.

children.name: This is the great truth.
children.full_sentence: This is the great truth.

children.name: Tapaniya Upanishad
Afterwards adjust the very gross Virat form in the very micro Hiranyagarbha form, and this micro for...
children.full_sentence: Afterwards adjust the very gross Virat form in the very micro Hiranyagarbha form, and this micro form in the great causal Iswara form and after arranging the mantras similarly, and meditating on “Otha-Anuj-Jnathru-Anugna-Avikalpa” stages, and merging all this in to the Omkara (sound of Om) in Thuriya, we have to reach the Nirvikalpa Paramathma (formless great truth).

children.name: One who knows this, would be without a body, without sensory organs, without soul, without ignorance...
children.full_sentence: One who knows this, would be without a body, without sensory organs, without soul, without ignorance, with the form of Sat Chit Ananda (ever lasting bliss) and 1269 --- Page 1270 --- ATHARVA-VEDA would become as one who attained salvation (one who attains Swarajya).

children.name: Under Yama (forbearance) are ten: Ahimsa, Satya, Asteya, Brahmacharya, Daya, Arjava, Kshama, Dhriti,...
children.full_sentence: Under Yama (forbearance) are ten: Ahimsa, Satya, Asteya, Brahmacharya, Daya, Arjava, Kshama, Dhriti, Mitahara and Saucha.

children.name: Satya is the speaking of the truth that conduces to the well-being of creatures, through the actions...
children.full_sentence: Satya is the speaking of the truth that conduces to the well-being of creatures, through the actions of one’s mind, speech, or body.

children.name: Para-Brahma Upanishad
Hence the four states (waking, etc.,) are to be considered as a covering by four fingers and just as...
children.full_sentence: Hence the four states (waking, etc.,) are to be considered as a covering by four fingers and just as the sacred thread is of ninety-six four-finger breadths (in extent) so the inward brahma- sutra consists of ninety-six categories (tattvas).

children.name: Then this which extends from the navel to the Brahmarandhra and connected with the twenty-seven tatt...
children.full_sentence: Then this which extends from the navel to the Brahmarandhra and connected with the twenty-seven tattvas separately and possessing the three gunas is to be considered as one though they are seen with the characteristics of the Trinity.

children.name: Things made of clay are considered real (but) it is verbosity (born of ignorance); the transformatio...
children.full_sentence: Things made of clay are considered real (but) it is verbosity (born of ignorance); the transformation is a (mere) name; that it is clay alone is the truth.

children.name: The twenty-four tattvas constitute the threads.
children.full_sentence: The twenty-four tattvas constitute the threads.

children.name: The nine tattvas constitute the one transcendent Brahman, (but people) provide many paths (such as S...
children.full_sentence: The nine tattvas constitute the one transcendent Brahman, (but people) provide many paths (such as Sankhya, Yoga, etc.,) due to difference in approach.

children.name: possess) true knowledge which is rooted in consciousness, having threads in the form of true princip...
children.full_sentence: possess) true knowledge which is rooted in consciousness, having threads in the form of true principles (tattvas) and extending from the navel to the tip of the aperture in the crown of the head (Brahmarandhra).

children.name: Paramahamsa-Parivrajaka Upanishad
He shall bathe during the three prescribed periods, listen to an exposition of the Vedanta and pract...
children.full_sentence: He shall bathe during the three prescribed periods, listen to an exposition of the Vedanta and practise the Pranava; be well established in the path of (realizing) Brahman; merge his favourite desire in the Atman; become free of ‘mine-ness’ and get established in the Self; give up passion, anger, greed, delusion, intoxication, rivalry, false pride, pride, egotism, intolerance, arrogance, desires, hatred, gloating, impetuosity, ‘mine-ness’, etc.; possessed of wisdom and dispassion he shall turn a

children.name: This type of outside knowledge should be attained by truth, penance and other rules of life dictated...
children.full_sentence: This type of outside knowledge should be attained by truth, penance and other rules of life dictated by celibacy and by the paths shown by Vedanta.

children.name: Rama-Rahasya Upanishad
The wise knower of Truth declare that the confluence of the above two terms end in the meaning ‘asi’...
children.full_sentence: The wise knower of Truth declare that the confluence of the above two terms end in the meaning ‘asi’ (Are).

children.name: Rama-rahasya Upanishad
This is the Truth.
children.full_sentence: This is the Truth.

children.name: Sandilya Upanishad
A person possessed of Yama and Niyama, avoiding all company, having finished his course of study, de...
children.full_sentence: A person possessed of Yama and Niyama, avoiding all company, having finished his course of study, delighting in truth and virtue, having conquered (his) anger, being engaged in the service of his spiritual instructor and having been obedient to his parents and well instructed in all the religious practices and the knowledge of his order of life, should go to a sacred grove abounding in fruits, roots and water.

children.name: With eyes half closed and with a firm mind, fixing his eyes on the tip of his nose and becoming abso...
children.full_sentence: With eyes half closed and with a firm mind, fixing his eyes on the tip of his nose and becoming absorbed in the sun and moon, he after remaining thus unshaken (becomes conscious of) the thing which is resplendent, which is the supreme truth and which is beyond.

children.name: As camphor in fire and salt in water become absorbed, so also the mind becomes absorbed in the Tattv...
children.full_sentence: As camphor in fire and salt in water become absorbed, so also the mind becomes absorbed in the Tattva (Truth).

children.name: (Then) the fluctuations of Prana are prevented, when one has no longing for this mundane existence o...
children.full_sentence: (Then) the fluctuations of Prana are prevented, when one has no longing for this mundane existence or when he has gratified his desires therein - through the study of religious books, the company of good men, indifference (to enjoyments), practice and Yoga or long contemplation with intentness on any desired (higher) object or through practising one truth firmly.

children.name: In that place is Khechari-mudra situated and that is the seat of Truth.
children.full_sentence: In that place is Khechari-mudra situated and that is the seat of Truth.

children.name: Thus is the direct perception (of truth) attained.
children.full_sentence: Thus is the direct perception (of truth) attained.

children.name: Through the Samyama of Chitta in the eye, he gets a knowledge of all worlds; in the ear, a knowledge...
children.full_sentence: Through the Samyama of Chitta in the eye, he gets a knowledge of all worlds; in the ear, a knowledge of Yama (the god of death) world); in the sides of the ear, a knowledge of Nrriti-world; in the back of it (the ear), a knowledge of Varuna-world; in the left ear, a knowledge of Vayu-world; in the throat, a knowledge of Soma (moon) world; in the left eye, a knowledge of Shiva-world; in the head, a knowledge of Atala world; in the feet, a knowledge of Vitala world; in the ankles, a knowledge of N

children.name: Teach me the science of Brahman by which I shall obtain that which is most excellent." 1377 --- Page...
children.full_sentence: Teach me the science of Brahman by which I shall obtain that which is most excellent." 1377 --- Page 1378 --- ATHARVA-VEDA Atharvan replied: "O Sandilya, Brahman is Satya, Vijnana and Ananta in which all this (world) is interwoven, warp-wise and woof-wise, from which all originated and into which all are absorbed and which being known makes everything else known.

children.name: That which is one and non-dual, that which pervades everything like Akasa, which is extremely subtle...
children.full_sentence: That which is one and non-dual, that which pervades everything like Akasa, which is extremely subtle, without a blemish, actionless, Sat (be-ness) only, the essence, of the bliss of consciousness, beneficent, calm and immortal and which is beyond.

children.name: CHAPTER – III Then Sandilya questioned Atharvan thus: "From the Brahman that is OM, imperishable, ac...
children.full_sentence: CHAPTER – III Then Sandilya questioned Atharvan thus: "From the Brahman that is OM, imperishable, actionless, beneficial, Sat (be-ness) only and supreme, how dissolution this universe arise ?

children.name: Please solve me this doubt." Atharvan replied: "The Supreme Brahman, the Truth, is the 1378 --- Page...
children.full_sentence: Please solve me this doubt." Atharvan replied: "The Supreme Brahman, the Truth, is the 1378 --- Page 1379 --- ATHARVA-VEDA imperishable and the actionless.

children.name: That which is Satya, Vijnana and Ananda; That which is actionless, without any impurity, omnipresent...
children.full_sentence: That which is Satya, Vijnana and Ananda; That which is actionless, without any impurity, omnipresent, extremely subtle, having faces in every direction, undefinable and immortal - that is His Nishkala aspect.

children.name: That form of him as Dattatreya, who sports with his Sakti, who is kind to his devotees, who is brill...
children.full_sentence: That form of him as Dattatreya, who sports with his Sakti, who is kind to his devotees, who is brilliant as fire, resembling the petals or a red lotus and is of four hands, who is mild and shines sinlessly - this is His Sakala form." Then Sandilya questioned Atharvan, "O Lord, that which is Sat only and the essence of the bliss of consciousness - why is He called Parabrahman?"
